data.title: Is Zentalis (ZNTL) Using Azenosertib’s New Dose To Reframe Its Ovarian Cancer Strategy?
data.description:
• Zentalis Pharmaceuticals recently reported that a 400mg once-daily, 5-days-on/2-days-off regimen of azenosertib was selected as the optimal monotherapy dose for Cyclin E1-positive platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, following a prespecified interim analysis from the DENALI Part 2a Phase 2 trial.
  • This dose decision, supported by a differentiated response rate, comparable safety and plans for inclusion in both the pivotal DENALI Phase 2 and confirmatory ASPENOVA Phase 3 trials, is central to Zentalis’s pursuit of a potential accelerated approval pathway in this biomarker-selected population.

To be comfortable holding Zentalis today, you really need to believe that azenosertib can translate its Cyclin E1-focused ovarian cancer program into a real, approvable product before the cash burn and execution risk catch up with it. The new 400 mg dosing decision tightens the story: it simplifies the registrational path, keeps DENALI and ASPENOVA aligned, and helps justify the recent sharp share price move as more than just speculation. In the near term, the main catalyst remains DENALI progress toward the year-end 2026 topline readout, with Fast Track status and the expanded Part 2c cohort incrementally supporting the accelerated approval narrative.
